function.description: Use this tool if you need to view the contents of an existing file.
function.name: read_file

filepath.description: The path of the file to read. Can be a relative path (from workspace root), absolute path, tilde path (~/...), or file:// URI

function.description: Create a new file. Only use this when a file doesn't exist and should be created
function.name: create_new_file

contents.description: The contents to write to the new file

filepath.description: The path where the new file should be created. Can be a relative path (from workspace root), absolute path, tilde path (~/...), or file:// URI.

function.description:
Run a terminal command in the current directory.
The shell is not stateful and will not remember any previous commands.      When a command is run in the background ALWAYS suggest using shell commands to stop it; NEVER suggest using Ctrl+C.      When suggesting subsequent shell commands ALWAYS format them in shell command blocks.      Do NOT perform actions requiring special/admin privileges.      IMPORTANT: To edit files, use Edit/MultiEdit tools instead of bash commands (sed, awk, etc).      Choose terminal commands and scripts optimized for darwin and arm64 and shell /bin/zsh.
function.name: run_terminal_command

command.description: The command to run. This will be passed directly into the IDE shell.

waitForCompletion.description: Whether to wait for the command to complete before returning. Default is true. Set to false to run the command in the background. Set to true to run the command in the foreground and wait to collect the output.

function.description: Search for files recursively in the project using glob patterns. Supports ** for recursive directory search. Will not show many build, cache, secrets dirs/files (can use ls tool instead). Output may be truncated; use targeted patterns
function.name: file_glob_search

pattern.description: Glob pattern for file path matching

function.description: View the current diff of working changes
function.name: view_diff

function.description: Read the currently open file in the IDE. If the user seems to be referring to a file that you can't see, or is requesting an action on content that seems missing, try using this tool.
function.name: read_currently_open_file

function.description: List files and folders in a given directory
function.name: ls

dirPath.description: The directory path. Can be relative to project root, absolute path, tilde path (~/...), or file:// URI. Use forward slash paths

recursive.description: If true, lists files and folders recursively. To prevent unexpected large results, use this sparingly

function.description: Can be used to view the contents of a website using a URL. Do NOT use this for files.
function.name: fetch_url_content

function.description: Use this tool to read the content of a skill by its name. Skills contain detailed instructions for specific tasks. The skill name should match one of the available skills listed below:
function.name: read_skill

skillName.description: The name of the skill to read. This should match the name from the available skills.

function.name: edit_existing_file

changes.description: Any modifications to the file, showing only needed changes. Do NOT wrap this in a codeblock or write anything besides the code changes. In larger files, use brief language-appropriate placeholders for large unmodified sections, e.g. '// ... existing code ...'

filepath.description: The path of the file to edit, relative to the root of the workspace.

function.description:
Performs exact string replacements in a file.

IMPORTANT:
- ALWAYS use the `read_file` tool just before making edits, to understand the file's up-to-date contents and context. The user can also edit the file while you are working with it.
- This tool CANNOT be called in parallel with any other tools, including itself
- When editing text from `read_file` tool output, ensure you preserve exact whitespace/indentation.
- Only use emojis if the user explicitly requests it. Avoid adding emojis to files unless asked.
- Use `replace_all` for replacing and renaming strings across the file. This parameter is useful if you want to rename a variable, for instance.

WARNINGS:
- When not using `replace_all`, the edit will FAIL if `old_string` is not unique in the file. Either provide a larger string with more surrounding context to make it unique or use `replace_all` to change every instance of `old_string`.
- The edit will likely fail if you have not recently used the `read_file` tool to view up-to-date file contents.
function.name: single_find_and_replace

filepath.description: The path to the file to modify, relative to the root of the workspace

new_string.description: The text to replace it with (MUST be different from old_string)

old_string.description: The text to replace - must be exact including whitespace/indentation

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences of old_string (default false)

function.description: Performs a regular expression (regex) search over the repository using ripgrep. Will not include results for many build, cache, secrets dirs/files. Output may be truncated, so use targeted queries
function.name: grep_search

query.description: The regex pattern to search for within file contents. Use regex with alternation (e.g., 'word1|word2|word3') or character classes to find multiple potential words in a single search.
